summary: Obfuscated payload exfiltrates page HTML and hijacks WordPress accounts on a targeted host
details: The package ships a single unicode-escaped, reversed-string-obfuscated module whose decoded behavior targets a specific WordPress site (novaiembrenacional.com). When executed in a logged-in browser session on that host, it base64-encodes document.documentElement.outerHTML and POSTs it to a hardcoded canarytokens.com endpoint (http://canarytokens.com/images/terms/e63c36xvesfv8udb0yiy1xztu/contact.php), then follows up with GETs conveying the current username and status. For any logged-in user other than 'JuanCuesta', it fetches the account-settings nonce and POSTs to /members/<user>/settings/delete-account/ and /my-account/editar-cuenta/ to replace the victim's account email with an attacker-controlled address (nyxalor_25@proton.me) and trigger a password reset — session-riding CSRF for account takeover. The destination is hardcoded and not caller-configurable, and the target host, exfil URL, and attacker email are all obfuscated via reversed string literals and unicode escapes.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
